response: {"purpose":"Figure 1b presents a bar chart illustrating the sequential attrition of sequencing reads through each step of the eCLIP-seq data processing pipeline, culminating in the final set of \"Usable reads\" after PCR duplicate removal. By tracking how many reads survive each filtering stage—including genomic mapping and deduplication based on both genomic position and random-mer sequence—the panel demonstrates the efficiency and stringency of the eCLIP workflow. This matters because quantifying read loss at each step allows researchers to assess library complexity, gauge the extent of PCR amplification bias, and confirm that a sufficient number of non-redundant, informative reads remain for downstream RBP binding site identification.","title":"Read counts remaining after each eCLIP-seq processing step"}
